title: Fish-Flavored Eggplant (Yúxiāng Qiézi)

ingredients.title: Main
items: Vegetable oil – 400 ml (for frying, though only part will be absorbed)

ingredients.title: Seasoning Sauce (mix beforehand)
items: Cornstarch slurry – 1 tsp cornstarch + 2 tsp water

instruction.title: Prepare the Eggplant
steps: Cut eggplant into thick batons (about 6–7 cm long).
Toss lightly with a dusting of cornstarch (helps crisp texture and prevents oil soaking).

instruction.title: Shallow Fry
steps: Heat oil in a wok to 180°C.
Fry eggplant batons in batches until surface lightly golden and interior just softened (1–2 min).
Remove and drain on paper towel. Pour off excess oil, leaving 2 tbsp in the wok.

instruction.title: Build the Flavor Base
steps: On medium heat, fry minced pork until slightly crisp and browned.
Add garlic, ginger, scallion whites. Stir-fry until aromatic (about 20 sec).
Add Pixian Doubanjiang. Fry until red oil separates (~30 sec).

instruction.title: Combine with Sauce
steps: Stir the prepared seasoning sauce to recombine.
Pour into wok, bring to a simmer.
Adjust seasoning balance (should be tangy, savory, slightly sweet).

instruction.title: Coat Eggplant
steps: Add cornstarch slurry to thicken to a glossy coating consistency.

instruction.title: Finish & Plate
steps: Garnish with scallion greens and a drop of sesame oil.
Serve immediately with steamed jasmine rice.

chefTips: Use Chinese or Japanese eggplants for best texture and flavor.
Light cornstarch dusting and fast frying reduces oil absorption.
Authentic Pixian Doubanjiang is essential for real fish-fragrant flavor.
Balance vinegar and sugar at the end for signature sweet-sour profile.
Some chefs steam eggplant first, then flash-fry for a silkier texture.
